BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of new snow and stormy weather a dangerous avalanche situation will persist in some regions.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last three days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind and in areas close to the tree line, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.

On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place individual large and, in isolated cases, very large powder avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some regions over a wide area 50 to 90 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes. In particular at a distance from ridgelines as well as in places that are protected from the wind: In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of new snow and strong wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase. Danger level 4 (high) will be reached.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and wind, large surface-area wind slabs will form. The avalanche activity will gradually increase. Large to very large natural avalanches are possible over a wide area. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanches can in isolated cases reach very large size and in some places endanger transportation routes situated at relatively high altitudes.
Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The new snow and wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack in all aspects. The wind slabs are prone to triggering. Over a wide area they will increase in size additionally. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The wind will be moderate. Over a wide area light snowfall: High avalanche danger will still be encountered.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In the late morning the natural activity of avalanches will increase.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the strong northwesterly wind, the snow drift accumulations will increase in size from early morning. Even emptied starting zones will be replenished as a consequence of snowfall and wind. Here medium-sized and large natural dry avalanches are possible.
In the typical avalanche paths the avalanches can as before reach very large size and in isolated cases endanger transportation routes that are exposed. More powder avalanches are possible.
The conditions are treacherous for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities outside marked and open pistes. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches, including large ones.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 50 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1500 m. Over a wide area 30 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until the evening above approximately 1800 m. The wind will be strong over a wide area.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Some large and, in isolated cases, very large powder avalanches have been released on Tuesday.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The danger of natural avalanches will decrease.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In some localities up to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. In the course of the day danger level 4 (high) will be reached above approximately 1800 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes strong wind, large surface-area wind slabs will form in all aspects. As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses. The new snow will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1800 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size. Especially on very steep shady slopes occasionally large avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: In some localities up to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Thursday above approximately 1600 m. The new snow will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack. As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes strong southerly wind, avalanche prone wind slabs will form in all aspects. Field observations and weak layers in the old snowpack indicate poor snowpack stability in particular in the regions exposed to substantial precipitation.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of new snow and stormy weather a dangerous avalanche situation will persist in some regions.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last three days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind and in areas close to the tree line, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.

On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place medium-sized and large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some regions over a wide area 40 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.highlights: The conditions are very dangerous for winter sport activities outside marked and open pistes. Natural avalanches are to be expected in the second half of the day.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released very easily. Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow and the sometimes large wind slabs that are forming in all aspects can be released very easily above the tree line. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found also in areas close to the tree line. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

The prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase from midday. As a consequence of new snow and strong wind more frequent natural avalanches are to be expected. Avalanches can in some cases penetrate deep layers and reach large size, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes.

On very steep slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of rain this also applies at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday. 15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Thursday. The wind will be strong in some cases.
The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The meteorological conditions will bring about a substantial weakening of the snowpack in the afternoon. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
The off-piste conditions are very dangerous. Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall during the night. This applies in particular in the north.

BulletinMeasurements.highlights: A lot of snow will fall from early morning in some regions. Backcountry touring and other off-piste activities call for great restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger. Outside marked and open pistes a very precarious avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: In some localities over a wide area 30 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes above the tree line. The sometimes storm force wind will transport the new snow, especially at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain at elevated altitudes. On very steep slopes natural avalanches must be expected. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized and easily released. They can be released by small loads. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area over a wide area 30 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall.
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the new snow. In all aspects wind slabs will form. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
The snowpack remains generally prone to triggering.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area. The avalanche danger will persist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent. The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1700 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Avalanches can be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered and natural avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Large to very large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The wind will be strong. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and strong wind the size of the avalanche prone locations will increase in the late morning.
Caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Over a wide area 15 to 40 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1600 m. Over a wide area 10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Thursday above approximately 1600 m. The wind will be strong.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Isolated medium-sized to large natural dry avalanches have been released on Tuesday.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally, in particular on steep shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude.

BulletinMeasurements.highlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind. Natural avalanches are to be expected.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Outside marked and open pistes a very dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow and the sometimes large wind slabs that are forming in all aspects can be released very easily above the tree line. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found also in areas close to the tree line. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase from midday. As a consequence of new snow and strong wind more frequent natural avalanches are to be expected. Avalanches can in some cases penetrate deep layers and reach large size, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes.

On very steep slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of rain this also applies at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Thursday. The wind will be strong in some cases.
The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The meteorological conditions will bring about a substantial weakening of the snowpack in the afternoon. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
The off-piste conditions are very dangerous. Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

10 to 20 cm of snow will fall during the night. This applies in particular in the north, in the Tuxer Alps, in the Zillertal Alps and in the High Tauern.

BulletinMeasurements.highlights: Increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and strong wind. Natural avalanches are to be expected.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Outside marked and open pistes a very dangerous avalanche situation will prevail. The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh snow and the sometimes large wind slabs that are forming in all aspects can be released very easily above the tree line. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found also in areas close to the tree line. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase from midday. As a consequence of new snow and strong wind more frequent natural avalanches are to be expected. Avalanches can in some cases penetrate deep layers and reach large size, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes.

On very steep slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized loose snow avalanches are possible, in the event of rain this also applies at low and intermediate altitudes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
15 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Thursday. The wind will be strong in some cases.
The wind will transport the new snow. Further wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering over a wide area. The meteorological conditions will bring about a substantial weakening of the snowpack in the afternoon. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Somewhat older wind slabs are lying on surface hoar. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment:
The off-piste conditions are very dangerous. Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

10 to 20 cm of snow will fall during the night. This applies in particular in the north, in the Tuxer Alps, in the Zillertal Alps and in the High Tauern.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Down to low altitudes snow will fall over a wide area. The wind will be strong. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The sometimes strong wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations will form by the early morning. These will form in particular above approximately 2000 m.

In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in areas close to the tree line.

In addition the hard wind slabs of the last few days in pass areas in all aspects and generally at elevated altitudes are capable of being triggered now as before. This applies in particular in case of a large load. The older wind slabs will be covered with new snow and therefore difficult to recognise.

Several medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some localities 5 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Thursday above approximately 1500 m. The strong wind will transport the fresh and old snow significantly.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Western Prealps and along the border with France: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes. In some places new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible. Mostly small natural avalanches are possible below approximately 1800 m.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: Up to 20 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1500 m. The wind will be strong to storm force. In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In some places wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line.

Restraint is advisable.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall from midday. The wind will be strong to storm force in some localities.
The sometimes storm force wind will transport the new snow. Sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form.

The snowpack will be in some cases prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.
